source_inputs.label: Operator brief
source_inputs.value: Create a WorthScan post for a used electric guitar listing with brand and condition details.

source_inputs.label: Valuation method
source_inputs.value: Check brand/model, pickups, neck condition, fret wear, included case, modifications, serial visibility if approved, and three local instrument comps.

source_inputs.label: Research boundary
source_inputs.value: Use reviewed manual trend notes only. Do not scrape music marketplaces or social platforms.

trend_examples.hook: This guitar price depends on the headstock
trend_examples.notes: Brand/model reveal supports music gear valuation.
Included accessories provide a simple value driver.

trend_examples.hook: Do not skip the neck and frets

notes: No paid generation or live research providers.

slides.on_screen_text: Scan this guitar before you meet up
slides.visual_direction: Show approved listing image, asking price, and model clue.

slides.on_screen_text: Check brand, model, pickups, neck, and frets
slides.visual_direction: Detail crop headstock, pickups, neck joint, frets, and case.
Show comp cards with brand, model, condition, and accessories.

slides.on_screen_text: Subtract fret wear, setup work, and missing case
Worth range: jam, negotiate, or pass
slides.visual_direction: Show final range, confidence, and CTA.

output_requirements.caption: Would you buy this guitar? Comment "scan" with the next listing. WorthScan gives a resale estimate, not a guarantee.
output_requirements.spoken_script: Before buying a used guitar, check the exact model, pickups, neck, frets, included case, and modifications. Compare three similar local listings and subtract setup or repair risk before calling the range.
posting_notes: Do not show full serial numbers unless approved and necessary.
Do not claim authenticity or repair status without human evidence.
